academic.rounding_note: No rounding off is permitted. Exactly 45% means 45 marks out of 100, or 135 out of 300, or 67.5 out of 150. (Clause 6.2.8)
academic.diploma_alternative: 10+3 Diploma examination with Mathematics as a compulsory subject and at least 45% aggregate marks is also accepted. (Clause 6.2.3(a))

aptitude.required_test: National Aptitude Test in Architecture (NATA), conducted by the Council of Architecture (CoA)
aptitude.relaxation_note: There is no relaxation in the minimum NATA passing score for any category. (Council of Architecture letter No. CA/5/Academic-NATA dated 11.03.2008, cited in Clause 6.2.3(b))
aptitude.no_keam_arch_exam_note: KEAM does NOT conduct an entrance exam for Architecture. KEAM only handles centralised allotment (CAP). For B.Arch, your eligibility is decided by NATA and your 10+2 marks.

age.upper_age_limit: There is no upper age limit for B.Arch admission. (Clause 6.3.1)

merit_formula.description: Architecture rank = NATA score (out of 200) + qualifying exam marks (out of 200), totalling 400. (Clause 9.7.4(c))
tiebreaker: Higher NATA score is placed higher
If still tied, higher Mathematics marks in the qualifying exam wins
If still tied, the older candidate is placed higher
If still tied, the decision of the Commissioner for Entrance Examinations is final

no_lateral_entry: There is no provision for lateral admission to the second year (or any later stage) of the 5-year B.Arch course. (Clause 6.2.3(c))

rules.title: 10+2 with Physics + Mathematics + 45% aggregate
rules.description: Physics and Mathematics are compulsory. The third subject can be Chemistry, Biology, Computer Science, IT, Informatics Practices, Engineering Graphics, Business Studies, or a Technical Vocational subject. Minimum 45% aggregate marks (no rounding).

rules.title: 10+3 Diploma with Maths is also accepted
rules.description: Candidates who passed a 10+3 Diploma examination with Mathematics as a compulsory subject and at least 45% aggregate marks are also eligible.

rules.title: Valid NATA 2025 or NATA 2026 score
rules.description: You must score at least the minimum eligibility marks specified in the NATA Information Brochure. The minimum is set by the Council of Architecture; KEAM has no power to relax it for any category.

rules.title: Minimum age 17 by 31 December 2026
rules.description: Candidates must complete 17 years of age on or before 31.12.2026. There is no relaxation in minimum age, and no upper age limit.

rules.title: Nativity for Keralite category
rules.description: Candidates claiming the Keralite category must produce nativity proof as defined in Clause 6.1. Non-Keralites can apply but compete for fewer reserved seats.

rules.title: No lateral entry to year 2
rules.description: B.Arch is a 5-year integrated programme. There is no provision to enter at a later stage by lateral admission.
